figures.caption: Fig. 5 Our framework for low-light image enhancement. We first estimate an illumination map for guiding the restoration process. The illumination map can help prevent over-exposure and under-exposure problems.

Fig. 1 First row : A comparison reveals that existing low-light enhancement method DLN [31] and restoration method GDNet [18] fail in dealing with complex night scene images. A cascade of separate restoration methods cannot work harmoniously and may even amplify the degradation pattern, where → means applying the two methods sequentially. To address the problem, we synthesize diverse datasets for end-to-end training. Second row : When applied to real-world images, former methods retrained on our dataset still exhibit over-exposure problem, including DLN [31], DDMSNet [42] and diffusion-based solutions (WeatherDiff [22] and DiT [23]). In contrast, our approach yields satisfactory results with the help of illuminance guidance.
Fig. 2 EC-Zero-DCE Curves with different adjustment parameters exposure e. The horizontal axis represents the input pixel values while the vertical axis represents the output pixel values.

Fig. 4 Our framework for low-light image enhancement. We first estimate an illumination map for guiding the restoration process. The illumination map can help prevent over-exposure and under-exposure problems.
